steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: Treat the two function values as points: $f(0)=9$ gives $(0,9)$, and $f(3)=9$ gives $(3,9)$. Since $f$ is linear, use those two points directly. The slope is $m=\frac{9-9}{3-0}=0.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=9$.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: The calculated result $m=0$ means the line is horizontal. With $b=9$, the function is $f(x)=9$. This also matches the quick mental check: both given inputs output the same value, so a linear function through both points must be constant.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=0$ — gives $f(0)=0$, not $9$. Eliminated.
  • B) $f(x)=3$ — gives $f(0)=3$, not $9$. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=9$ — gives $f(0)=9$ and $f(3)=9$. Matches.
  • D) $f(x)=x+9$ — gives $f(3)=12$, not $9$. Eliminated.
That confirms C is correct.
